title: Vittadinia pterochaeta
taxonomy: Vittadinia pterochaeta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to the southeastern region of Australia. Vittadinia pterochaeta is a member of the genus Vittadinia, which comprises about 50 species of Australian plants. The plant is also known as New South Wales silver daisy bush due to its silvery undersides of leaves and daisy-like flowers. Vittadinia pterochaeta is considered to be a shrub or small tree, typically growing up to 3 meters in height.
